sections.cool_tools:
### Cool Tools & Models
**[AI That Spots Your Secret Online Life]: AI | The Verge**  
New AI tools are getting really good at figuring out who owns anonymous accounts on sites like Reddit or X, by analyzing writing styles and patterns — kind of like a digital detective matching fingerprints. It's cool (and a bit scary) because it shows how AI can connect the dots on online privacy, which matters if you have alt accounts for fun or venting. Teens who love social media should check this out to thi
sections.try_this:
### Try This at Home
- Open notebooklm.google.com on your phone or laptop, upload a few pages from a school article or your notes, and generate a cinematic video overview — you'll see your ideas turn into an animated story, showing how AI can make studying feel like watching a movie!
- Head to chat.openai.com and ask ChatGPT to "analyze my writing style from this sample text" (paste something you've written anonymously online) — compare it to the real you and think about online privacy like in t
sections.quick_bits:
### Quick Bits
**[AI Drama: Companies Calling Out Lies]**  
Anthropic's CEO accused OpenAI of lying about their military AI deals, after Anthropic backed out of a similar contract over safety concerns — it's like a behind-the-scenes feud in the AI world showing how companies wrestle with ethics. This is interesting because it reminds us AI isn't just fun tools; there are real debates about using it responsibly, especially in serious stuff like defense.  
Source: https://techcrunch.com/2026/03/04
